backend.main.validate_external_test_url.docstring:
Reject anything that isn't a plain public http(s) URL before this server fetches
it with a real browser. Without this, a "test my product URL" feature is a built-in
SSRF tool: it would let any caller make this server's network position issue requests
to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints (e.g. 169.254.169.254), or localhost.

backend.main.ensure_playwright_chromium_.docstring:
Lazily install the Playwright Chromium browser if the build-time install didn't take effect.

Render's build step is expected to install this (see render-build.sh), but build-time
browser caching has been observed to drift from what the deployed Python process actually
finds at runtime. This is a one-shot-per-process fallback so a stale/missing browser cache
doesn't permanently break the feature until the next manual redeploy. Emits periodic
heartbeats via `report` since the download can take 1-3 minutes with no other output,
which otherwise looks like a frozen/hung job to anyone watching the status line.

backend.main.test_suite_nextjs_route_pat.docstring:
Next.js App Router infers the route path from the file's directory,
e.g. app/api/users/route.ts -> /api/users. Generic string transform, not
tied to any specific repo.

backend.main.extract_test_suite_routes.docstring:
Backend route declarations (method+path) plus frontend-perceived call targets.

Scans each analyzed repo's own cached source files directly — not the SCIM
`code` chunks, which start at the `def`/function line and strip any
`@decorator` line above it, so a decorator-only regex against chunk code
never matches. The source root is resolved per owner/repo via the same
`artifact_root_for_output` + `source_root_for_artifact` helpers every other
analysis feature in this app uses, so this generalizes to any repo a user
analyzes — nothing about a specific repo or path is hardcoded here.
Frontend-perceived endpoints already extracted into html_features.json
(form actions, fetch()/axios calls) are appended too, since those cover
routes whose backend declaration may live outside this analyzed repo.

backend.main.extract_test_suite_ui_eleme.docstring:
Real DOM elements (buttons/links/inputs/forms) with selectors, reusing the
html_ui_graph.json artifact already built by build_html_ui_graph() during
analysis — the same elements the Code Map's HTML UI interaction graph uses.

backend.main.extract_test_suite_ui_flows.docstring:
UI-element -> target edges (form submit, click handler, link, fetch/axios
call) straight from the same html_ui_graph.json edge_details used to draw
the Code Map's HTML UI interaction graph.

backend.main.extract_test_suite_error_si.docstring:
Functions the existing static-quality scanner already flagged as having
no explicit error handling (the `no_error_handling` quality_finding type) —
good candidates for "does this endpoint fail gracefully" tests.

backend.main.generate_test_suite_code_ch.docstring:
Code-generation analog of generate_search_answer_from_prompt(), tuned for
Playwright test code instead of a natural-language answer: a larger
response token budget (generated code is verbose) and markdown-fence
stripping, since models sometimes wrap code output in ```python blocks
even when told not to.

backend.main.test_suite_static_boilerpla.docstring:
Generic, repo-independent tests from the spec's "ALWAYS INCLUDE" list.
Hardcoded rather than LLM-generated since they don't need per-repo
customization beyond the target URL — this also saves tokens/cost.

backend.main.restore_latest_analysis_art.docstring:
Restore local artifacts from the latest completed analysis run for a
known GitHub user + repository, without needing a live request/session.

Shared by /search's restore (session-derived user) and /analyze's
restore-before-rebuild (user derived from the request session or, for
background jobs, from the github_context captured at job launch).

Restore local artifacts required by /search from the latest saved run.

Dashboard restore can display signed Supabase URLs without local files, but
server-side search needs local SCIM/function metadata and graph JSON.

backend.main.write_ga_user_actions_scim_.docstring: Write GA behavior as searchable source text so SCIM embeds observed user actions.

backend.main.codeval_secret_encryption_k.docstring:
Shared symmetric key for at-rest encryption of third-party integration
credentials (Mixpanel service account secret, Sentry auth token, etc.).

backend.main.write_mixpanel_user_actions.docstring: Write Mixpanel activity as searchable source text, mirroring write_ga_user_actions_scim_document.

backend.main.sentry_issue_event_frames.docstring:
Fetch the latest event for an issue and return its exception stack frames,
ordered most-relevant-first (Sentry returns frames oldest -> newest; the
crash site is the last frame, and in_app frames are more likely to be
the user's own code than library frames).

backend.main.sentry_match_event_to_code.docstring:
Match by filename/abs_path first, then function_name, walking the stack
frames if the top frame doesn't resolve to anything in the index.

backend.main.file_has_directive_framewor.docstring:
True if a JS/TS file opens with a `"use server"`/`"use client"` directive.
Next.js treats every export in such a file as a framework-invoked entry point
(a Server Action or a client boundary), so the absence of static incoming edges
from other parsed code is expected, not a sign of dead/orphaned code.

backend.main.ast_missing_none_check_find.docstring:
Flag `target = some_call()` immediately followed by `target.attr` with no
None/truthiness guard in between, restricted to call shapes that commonly return
None (dict.get, regex match/search, next/getattr with no default).

backend.main.python_ast_additional_bug_f.docstring:
Additional AST-based scanners: bare/silent except handling, functions with no
error handling at all, likely-missing None checks, deprecated stdlib imports,
oversized functions, and FastAPI routes with no detected auth dependency.

backend.main.quality_signal_opaque_error.docstring:
Detect telltale signs that the only error evidence is an opaque, redacted
payload (e.g. a Next.js production error `digest`) rather than a real exception
message or stack trace, so the LLM is told explicitly not to over-attribute root
cause to a specific function from this evidence alone.

backend.main.quality_signal_snippets_are.docstring:
True if every snippet came from the whole-file fallback in self_heal_code_snippets
(no exact function/symbol match was found for the finding's mapped node names) —
meaning the file is plausibly relevant but no specific function was actually verified.

backend.main.deterministic_no_evidence_q.docstring:
Hard, non-LLM-bypassable guard: when zero source code was retrieved for this
finding's mapped node(s) AND the only error evidence is an opaque/redacted payload
(e.g. a Mixpanel-mapped Next.js production error digest, with no real file_path
ever populated upstream), do not let an LLM call invent a verdict or a fix from
nothing. This is the exact gap that let a fabricated Express-style (req, res) patch
get generated for a Next.js Server Component with no req/res in scope.

backend.main.generate_callgraph_with_pya.docstring:
Generate a Python call graph using pyan3, save DOT, JSON, and interactive HTML,
with numbering resetting for each top-level node.

Generate a Python call graph using pyan3 from Python code, save DOT, JSON, and interactive HTML.

Args:
    repo_dir (str): Directory containing Python files.
    dot_file (str, optional): Path to save DOT output. Defaults to repo_dir/callgraph_pyan.dot.
    html_file (str, optional): Path to save interactive HTML. Defaults to repo_dir/callgraph_pyan.html.
    json_file (str, optional): Path to save JSON version of callgraph. Defaults to repo_dir/callgraph_pyan.json.

backend.main.load_ast_from_json.docstring:
Load the AST dump string from your .ast.json file and reconstruct
the Python AST node.

backend.main.export_call_graph_json.docstring: Export the NetworkX graph to a JSON file.

backend.main.build_python_callgraph.docstring:
Build a lightweight Python callgraph from AST.
Keeps only calls that resolve to user-defined functions/methods in the repo.

backend.main.build_call_graph_from_ast_f.docstring: Build a call graph from every supported AST JSON in a folder.

backend.main.build_fast_javalang_callgra.docstring:
Build Java callgraph using javalang parser.
Saves callgraph_fast.json with actual caller -> callee edges.

backend.main.trim_callgraph_with_ast.docstring:
Trim callgraph.json to keep only user-defined functions.
Remove synthetic/internal calls like <module>, <iterator>, <lambda>0, <metaClassCallHandler>.

backend.main.analyze_code.docstring:
JSON-safe, libcst-based analyzer.
Produces per-function call tracking for call graph construction.

backend.main.extract_zip.docstring:
Safe ZIP extraction for GitHub repos.
Handles:
- missing parent dirs
- illegal Windows characters
- deep nested paths
- zip-slip protection
- overly long path components

backend.main.trim_callgraph_and_cfg.docstring:
Keep only user-defined functions in callgraph.json.
Remove calls to external functions, built-ins, or libraries.

backend.main.run_joern.docstring:
Run Joern analysis via Scala script for Java code.
Produces:
    functions.json
    callgraph.json
    cfg.json
    roots.json
    comments.json

backend.main.extract_query_tokens.docstring:
Extract all tokens from a natural-language query that match
any function name, fullName, or keyword.

backend.main.build_generic_cytoscape_gra.docstring:
nodes: list of function fullNames
edges: list of [caller, callee] or [caller, callee, order, line]
output_repo_dir: e.g. C:/DATA/Code/Progduct/output/owner_repo/
graph_name: e.g. "search_graph"

backend.main.find_callers.docstring: Return all nodes in the callgraph that call `symbol`.

backend.main.feature_explanation_resolve.docstring:
Find callgraph node names matching a free-text target. Tries the exact/tail
match used elsewhere in the codebase first; feature-level concepts (e.g.
"checkout") are often a module segment rather than a function tail
(app.routes.checkout.start_checkout), so fall back to substring containment
against the dotted path when the precise match finds nothing.

backend.main.feature_explanation_symbol_.docstring: Fall back to the SCIM symbol index when no callgraph nodes match the target.

backend.main.feature_explanation_node_lo.docstring:
Resolve callgraph node names to {path, line} via the SCIM symbol index, for
building file lists and showing readers where each step actually lives.

backend.main.handle_feature_explanation.docstring:
Handler for the "feature_explanation" query type: explain HOW something
works, grounded in callgraph traversal (5 levels forward, 3 back) plus
recent commit history, instead of a single-hop location lookup.

backend.main.match_feature_catalog_entry.docstring:
Match a 'where is feature X implemented' question against the repo's catalogued
feature names before falling back to generic text/symbol search. Without this, a
question naming a feature by its UI display name (e.g. "Ask CodeVal") can collide
with unrelated identifiers that merely share a substring with the product name.

backend.main.search_result_graph.docstring:
Build a callgraph subgraph centered on a single search result.

Used when the user clicks a specific result in the search results list
(as opposed to /search, which auto-picks the first graph-resolvable
result out of the whole ranked list).

backend.main.compact_feature_for_llm.docstring: Keep only feature-relevant evidence for user-facing descriptions.

backend.main.feature_generation_controls.docstring: Controls allowed during LLM generation; excludes user descriptions.

backend.main.fetch_repo_info.docstring:
Fetch metadata for a GitHub repo.
Returns (repo_info_json, default_branch) or raises an exception.

backend.main.run_pycg.docstring:
Run the unified Python analysis pipeline.

Uses:
- AST metadata extraction
- PyCG semantic analysis
- merged function graph
- file graph
- per-file graphs

backend.main.download_github_remote_sour.docstring:
Build a sparse local source tree from read-only GitHub tree/blob and raw content calls.
This is not a checkout; it downloads only filtered source/text blobs one file at a time.

backend.main.code_md_endpoint.docstring:
Return a CODE.md file for a GitHub repository.

If the repository has already been analyzed by CodeVal the artifacts are
loaded from disk immediately (fast).  If not, a 404 is returned with
instructions to run /analyze first — keeping this endpoint synchronous and
avoiding unbounded analysis time inside a single HTTP request.

backend.main.build_code_md_artifact.docstring: Build Code.md from deterministic repo evidence only.

backend.main.local_path_repo_identity.docstring:
Deterministic per-folder id (not a random uuid) so repeated analyses of
the same workspace land in the same output_repo_dir and can reuse
cached_analyze_results instead of always rebuilding from scratch.

backend.main.analyze_local_path.docstring:
Analyze a folder already on this machine's disk in place — used by the
VS Code extension, which runs this server locally. Skips the zip/upload/
extract round trip that /analyze/upload needs for payloads coming over
the network from a different machine.

backend.main.analyze_local_path_start.docstring:
Job-based variant of /analyze/local_path — starts the analysis on a
background thread and returns a job_id to poll via /analyze/status/{job_id},
matching the existing GitHub-repo analyze flow's contract.

backend.scim.TfidfProjector.docstring: Corpus-fitted TF-IDF embeddings projected to a compact dense vector.

backend.scim.chunk_search_document.docstring: Build the searchable document for one code unit.

backend.scim.simplify_symbol.docstring: Normalize extractor-specific symbols to the Class.method shape.

backend.scim.derive_feature_catalog_from.docstring:
Derive feature names from the built SCIM index records.

This intentionally does NOT use product-type feature templates.  The old
implementation leaked hardcoded game labels like "movement and collisions"
into unrelated repos.  Here a feature is derived from evidence already in
functions.jsonl: symbols, paths, code tokens, graph degrees, anchor type,
and fused embeddings when available.

backend.scim.record_index_terms.docstring: Terms from the SCIM index row, weighted toward real code symbols.

backend.scim.feature_key_from_record.docstring: Pick a stable grouping key from a real SCIM record, not a product template.

backend.scim.human_facing_feature_hypoth.docstring:
Extract repo-agnostic feature hypotheses from user-facing text.

UI/product repos usually expose features through labels, headings, docs,
routes, examples, or README text.  Library repos expose them through public
APIs and examples.  This function only creates hypotheses; implementation
evidence is attached later from code records.

backend.scim.record_feature_group_key.docstring: Choose the feature cluster key from file/class/route evidence first.

backend.features.core.helpers.callgraph_.docstring:
Build forward (caller -> callees) and backward (callee -> callers)
adjacency dicts from either load_search_callgraph() shape: a legacy
{caller: [callees]} dict, or the modern list of {"method", "calls": [...]}.

backend.features.core.helpers.traverse_f.docstring: Walk what `start_nodes` call, up to max_depth levels deep.

backend.features.core.helpers.traverse_b.docstring: Walk what calls `start_nodes`, up to max_depth levels deep.

backend.features.core.helpers.get_impact.docstring:
Blast radius for a modified function: everything that transitively
depends on it (direct callers, their callers, ...), unbounded depth but
capped at max_nodes. Each reached node is annotated with a confidence
bucket: "high" if it's reached via at least one directly-parsed call edge
(has order/line metadata), "low" if every edge on its shortest path here
is a heuristic, name-matched edge (e.g. the UI alias edges
add_ui_alias_edges() adds by string-matching a handler name — those carry
no order/line, since they aren't from an actual traced call site).

backend.features.core.helpers._python_mo.docstring:
Mirrors module_name_for() in build_python_callgraph (main.py): turn a
repo-relative .py path into the dotted module name used in callgraph
node ids, so get_function_spans() symbols line up with real graph nodes.

backend.features.core.helpers.get_functi.docstring:
Return [{"symbol", "start_line", "end_line"}] for every function/method
defined in file_path, using the same dotted module.Class.func naming
convention as build_python_callgraph()'s PythonCallVisitor in main.py.

Pass `source` to parse in-memory content (e.g. from `git show
<rev>:<path>`) instead of reading file_path off disk — that's how the
deletion report gets the "before" side of a diff without needing any
snapshot cache. Returns None when the file's language isn't supported
yet (only .py today), and [] when it's a supported language with zero
functions or a parse error — callers must not conflate the two.

backend.features.feature_detection.featu.docstring:
Extract candidate feature names from the UI text already pulled from the
analyzed repo's HTML / dashboard / docs.

Priority order:
  1. ui_text_items  — nav labels, headings, button text from HTML files
  2. readme_items   — markdown headings (## Feature Name)
  3. document_items — first lines of doc/spec files

Returns up to 40 de-duplicated candidate feature names, short enough to be
real nav/heading labels (3-60 chars), with junk lines filtered out.

Extract product feature names from user-facing HTML/UI text only.

README/docs/comments are supporting evidence, not feature-name sources.
They are only allowed through an explicit product_features fallback when
no UI labels exist.

Build the SCIM search index and (optionally) the feature catalog for an analyzed repo.

Key changes vs old version:
  - Safety guard: refuses to index PROJECT_ROOT or any parent of it.
  - Extracts UI feature seeds BEFORE calling feature_catalog_payload.
  - Passes seeds via repo_context so the catalog uses UI names as anchors.

build_feature_catalog=False skips UI-seed extraction and the feature
catalog/evidence/manifest steps entirely, building only the SCIM vector
search index (vectors.sqlite). Callers that don't want feature-detection
output (e.g. the local plugin analysis path) pass this to get search
without the heuristic, often-approximate feature catalog.

Call the LLM to generate user-facing descriptions for each feature.

Key change: when ui_feature_seeds are supplied the LLM is told those names
ARE the feature taxonomy — it explains each feature using callgraph evidence
rather than discovering/renaming features from function name patterns.

Each feature dict is expected to have at least:
  {
    "name": str,           # detected feature name (may be fn-name based)
    "summary": str,        # brief auto-summary
    "functions": [...],    # matching callgraph functions
    "evidence": str,       # comment/doc evidence text
  }

Returns the same list with a "description" key added to each item.

Generate user-facing descriptions in small paid LLM batches.

The full extracted feature list can be large. This function intentionally
sends only feature names plus tiny implementation hints to avoid TPM errors.

backend.features.feature_detection.ui.ht.docstring: Generic fallback for client-rendered UIs with section config objects.

Return UI sections in document order.

Algorithm:
1. Parse HTML with BeautifulSoup and ignore non-visible implementation tags.
2. Walk elements top-to-bottom.
3. Treat semantic containers, ARIA regions, titled cards/panels, forms,
   dialogs, navs, tabs, and similar blocks as candidate feature sections.
4. Use the container's own heading/legend/summary/ARIA/data title as the
   feature name.
5. Do not emit broad parents when their direct children are already
   labelled sections.
6. Keep nested controls as context, not as separate feature names.

Compatibility API used by the HTML UI graph builder.

Returns generic UI feature records extracted from every HTML-like file under
repo_root. The shape keeps legacy `buttons` and `routes` keys while exposing
section-level `features` from the current parser.

scripts.deletion-report.git_prefix.docstring: Path from the Git toplevel to repo_root, with a trailing slash.

scripts.deletion-report.changed_files.docstring:
[(status, old_rel_path, new_rel_path, old_git_path, new_git_path)].

old/new_rel_path are relative to repo_root for filesystem reads and
report output; old/new_git_path are relative to the Git toplevel for
git show/diff pathspecs.

status is one of A(dded)/M(odified)/D(eleted)/R(enamed). Uses -M so a
detected rename carries its old path through (so a renamed file's
symbols get diffed old-content vs new-content, not reported as a full
delete-then-add).

scripts.deletion-report.git_show.docstring: Content of rel_path at `base`, or None if it didn't exist there.

scripts.deletion-report.changed_old_line.docstring:
[(start, end)] line ranges in the OLD (base) version of rel_path that
this diff touches — parsed from unified=0 hunk headers, used to confirm a
symbol whose name survived the edit was actually touched, not just
re-emitted identically.

scripts.deletion-report.diff_file_symbol.docstring:
Returns (deleted, added, confirmed_modified, pre_spans_by_symbol) for
one changed file. pre_spans_by_symbol carries start/end lines so callers
can report file+line for deleted symbols.

backend.main.load_ast_from_json.docstring:
Load the AST dump string from your .ast.json file and reconstruct
the Python AST node.

backend.main.export_call_graph_json.docstring: Export the NetworkX graph to a JSON file.

backend.main.build_python_callgraph.docstring:
Build a lightweight Python callgraph from AST.
Keeps only calls that resolve to user-defined functions/methods in the repo.

backend.main.build_call_graph_from_ast_f.docstring: Build a call graph from every supported AST JSON in a folder.

backend.main.build_fast_javalang_callgra.docstring:
Build Java callgraph using javalang parser.
Saves callgraph_fast.json with actual caller -> callee edges.

backend.main.trim_callgraph_with_ast.docstring:
Trim callgraph.json to keep only user-defined functions.
Remove synthetic/internal calls like <module>, <iterator>, <lambda>0, <metaClassCallHandler>.

backend.main.analyze_code.docstring:
JSON-safe, libcst-based analyzer.
Produces per-function call tracking for call graph construction.

backend.main.extract_zip.docstring:
Safe ZIP extraction for GitHub repos.
Handles:
- missing parent dirs
- illegal Windows characters
- deep nested paths
- zip-slip protection
- overly long path components

backend.main.trim_callgraph_and_cfg.docstring:
Keep only user-defined functions in callgraph.json.
Remove calls to external functions, built-ins, or libraries.

backend.main.run_joern.docstring:
Run Joern analysis via Scala script for Java code.
Produces:
    functions.json
    callgraph.json
    cfg.json
    roots.json
    comments.json

backend.main.extract_query_tokens.docstring:
Extract all tokens from a natural-language query that match
any function name, fullName, or keyword.

backend.main.build_generic_cytoscape_gra.docstring:
nodes: list of function fullNames
edges: list of [caller, callee] or [caller, callee, order, line]
output_repo_dir: e.g. C:/DATA/Code/Progduct/output/owner_repo/
graph_name: e.g. "search_graph"

backend.main.find_callers.docstring: Return all nodes in the callgraph that call `symbol`.

backend.main.feature_explanation_resolve.docstring:
Find callgraph node names matching a free-text target. Tries the exact/tail
match used elsewhere in the codebase first; feature-level concepts (e.g.
"checkout") are often a module segment rather than a function tail
(app.routes.checkout.start_checkout), so fall back to substring containment
against the dotted path when the precise match finds nothing.

backend.main.feature_explanation_symbol_.docstring: Fall back to the SCIM symbol index when no callgraph nodes match the target.

backend.main.feature_explanation_node_lo.docstring:
Resolve callgraph node names to {path, line} via the SCIM symbol index, for
building file lists and showing readers where each step actually lives.

backend.main.handle_feature_explanation.docstring:
Handler for the "feature_explanation" query type: explain HOW something
works, grounded in callgraph traversal (5 levels forward, 3 back) plus
recent commit history, instead of a single-hop location lookup.

backend.main.match_feature_catalog_entry.docstring:
Match a 'where is feature X implemented' question against the repo's catalogued
feature names before falling back to generic text/symbol search. Without this, a
question naming a feature by its UI display name (e.g. "Ask CodeVal") can collide
with unrelated identifiers that merely share a substring with the product name.

backend.main.search_result_graph.docstring:
Build a callgraph subgraph centered on a single search result.

Used when the user clicks a specific result in the search results list
(as opposed to /search, which auto-picks the first graph-resolvable
result out of the whole ranked list).

backend.main.compact_feature_for_llm.docstring: Keep only feature-relevant evidence for user-facing descriptions.

backend.main.feature_generation_controls.docstring: Controls allowed during LLM generation; excludes user descriptions.

backend.main.fetch_repo_info.docstring:
Fetch metadata for a GitHub repo.
Returns (repo_info_json, default_branch) or raises an exception.

backend.main.run_pycg.docstring:
Run the unified Python analysis pipeline.

Uses:
- AST metadata extraction
- PyCG semantic analysis
- merged function graph
- file graph
- per-file graphs

backend.main.download_github_remote_sour.docstring:
Build a sparse local source tree from read-only GitHub tree/blob and raw content calls.
This is not a checkout; it downloads only filtered source/text blobs one file at a time.

backend.main.code_md_endpoint.docstring:
Return a CODE.md file for a GitHub repository.

If the repository has already been analyzed by CodeVal the artifacts are
loaded from disk immediately (fast).  If not, a 404 is returned with
instructions to run /analyze first — keeping this endpoint synchronous and
avoiding unbounded analysis time inside a single HTTP request.

backend.main.build_code_md_artifact.docstring: Build Code.md from deterministic repo evidence only.

backend.main.local_path_repo_identity.docstring:
Deterministic per-folder id (not a random uuid) so repeated analyses of
the same workspace land in the same output_repo_dir and can reuse
cached_analyze_results instead of always rebuilding from scratch.

backend.main.analyze_local_path.docstring:
Analyze a folder already on this machine's disk in place — used by the
VS Code extension, which runs this server locally. Skips the zip/upload/
extract round trip that /analyze/upload needs for payloads coming over
the network from a different machine.

backend.main.analyze_local_path_start.docstring:
Job-based variant of /analyze/local_path — starts the analysis on a
background thread and returns a job_id to poll via /analyze/status/{job_id},
matching the existing GitHub-repo analyze flow's contract.

backend.scim.TfidfProjector.docstring: Corpus-fitted TF-IDF embeddings projected to a compact dense vector.

backend.scim.chunk_search_document.docstring: Build the searchable document for one code unit.

backend.scim.simplify_symbol.docstring: Normalize extractor-specific symbols to the Class.method shape.

backend.scim.derive_feature_catalog_from.docstring:
Derive feature names from the built SCIM index records.

This intentionally does NOT use product-type feature templates.  The old
implementation leaked hardcoded game labels like "movement and collisions"
into unrelated repos.  Here a feature is derived from evidence already in
functions.jsonl: symbols, paths, code tokens, graph degrees, anchor type,
and fused embeddings when available.

backend.scim.record_index_terms.docstring: Terms from the SCIM index row, weighted toward real code symbols.

backend.scim.feature_key_from_record.docstring: Pick a stable grouping key from a real SCIM record, not a product template.

backend.scim.human_facing_feature_hypoth.docstring:
Extract repo-agnostic feature hypotheses from user-facing text.

UI/product repos usually expose features through labels, headings, docs,
routes, examples, or README text.  Library repos expose them through public
APIs and examples.  This function only creates hypotheses; implementation
evidence is attached later from code records.

backend.scim.record_feature_group_key.docstring: Choose the feature cluster key from file/class/route evidence first.

backend.features.core.helpers.callgraph_.docstring:
Build forward (caller -> callees) and backward (callee -> callers)
adjacency dicts from either load_search_callgraph() shape: a legacy
{caller: [callees]} dict, or the modern list of {"method", "calls": [...]}.

backend.features.core.helpers.traverse_f.docstring: Walk what `start_nodes` call, up to max_depth levels deep.

backend.features.core.helpers.traverse_b.docstring: Walk what calls `start_nodes`, up to max_depth levels deep.

backend.features.core.helpers.get_impact.docstring:
Blast radius for a modified function: everything that transitively
depends on it (direct callers, their callers, ...), unbounded depth but
capped at max_nodes. Each reached node is annotated with a confidence
bucket: "high" if it's reached via at least one directly-parsed call edge
(has order/line metadata), "low" if every edge on its shortest path here
is a heuristic, name-matched edge (e.g. the UI alias edges
add_ui_alias_edges() adds by string-matching a handler name — those carry
no order/line, since they aren't from an actual traced call site).

backend.features.core.helpers._python_mo.docstring:
Mirrors module_name_for() in build_python_callgraph (main.py): turn a
repo-relative .py path into the dotted module name used in callgraph
node ids, so get_function_spans() symbols line up with real graph nodes.

backend.features.core.helpers.get_functi.docstring:
Return [{"symbol", "start_line", "end_line"}] for every function/method
defined in file_path, using the same dotted module.Class.func naming
convention as build_python_callgraph()'s PythonCallVisitor in main.py.

Pass `source` to parse in-memory content (e.g. from `git show
<rev>:<path>`) instead of reading file_path off disk — that's how the
deletion report gets the "before" side of a diff without needing any
snapshot cache. Returns None when the file's language isn't supported
yet (only .py today), and [] when it's a supported language with zero
functions or a parse error — callers must not conflate the two.

backend.features.feature_detection.featu.docstring:
Extract candidate feature names from the UI text already pulled from the
analyzed repo's HTML / dashboard / docs.

Priority order:
  1. ui_text_items  — nav labels, headings, button text from HTML files
  2. readme_items   — markdown headings (## Feature Name)
  3. document_items — first lines of doc/spec files

Returns up to 40 de-duplicated candidate feature names, short enough to be
real nav/heading labels (3-60 chars), with junk lines filtered out.

Extract product feature names from user-facing HTML/UI text only.

README/docs/comments are supporting evidence, not feature-name sources.
They are only allowed through an explicit product_features fallback when
no UI labels exist.

Build the SCIM search index and (optionally) the feature catalog for an analyzed repo.

Key changes vs old version:
  - Safety guard: refuses to index PROJECT_ROOT or any parent of it.
  - Extracts UI feature seeds BEFORE calling feature_catalog_payload.
  - Passes seeds via repo_context so the catalog uses UI names as anchors.

build_feature_catalog=False skips UI-seed extraction and the feature
catalog/evidence/manifest steps entirely, building only the SCIM vector
search index (vectors.sqlite). Callers that don't want feature-detection
output (e.g. the local plugin analysis path) pass this to get search
without the heuristic, often-approximate feature catalog.

Call the LLM to generate user-facing descriptions for each feature.

Key change: when ui_feature_seeds are supplied the LLM is told those names
ARE the feature taxonomy — it explains each feature using callgraph evidence
rather than discovering/renaming features from function name patterns.

Each feature dict is expected to have at least:
  {
    "name": str,           # detected feature name (may be fn-name based)
    "summary": str,        # brief auto-summary
    "functions": [...],    # matching callgraph functions
    "evidence": str,       # comment/doc evidence text
  }

Returns the same list with a "description" key added to each item.

Generate user-facing descriptions in small paid LLM batches.

The full extracted feature list can be large. This function intentionally
sends only feature names plus tiny implementation hints to avoid TPM errors.

backend.features.feature_detection.ui.ht.docstring: Generic fallback for client-rendered UIs with section config objects.

Return UI sections in document order.

Algorithm:
1. Parse HTML with BeautifulSoup and ignore non-visible implementation tags.
2. Walk elements top-to-bottom.
3. Treat semantic containers, ARIA regions, titled cards/panels, forms,
   dialogs, navs, tabs, and similar blocks as candidate feature sections.
4. Use the container's own heading/legend/summary/ARIA/data title as the
   feature name.
5. Do not emit broad parents when their direct children are already
   labelled sections.
6. Keep nested controls as context, not as separate feature names.

Compatibility API used by the HTML UI graph builder.

Returns generic UI feature records extracted from every HTML-like file under
repo_root. The shape keeps legacy `buttons` and `routes` keys while exposing
section-level `features` from the current parser.

scripts.deletion-report.git_prefix.docstring: Path from the Git toplevel to repo_root, with a trailing slash.

scripts.deletion-report.changed_files.docstring:
[(status, old_rel_path, new_rel_path, old_git_path, new_git_path)].

old/new_rel_path are relative to repo_root for filesystem reads and
report output; old/new_git_path are relative to the Git toplevel for
git show/diff pathspecs.

status is one of A(dded)/M(odified)/D(eleted)/R(enamed). Uses -M so a
detected rename carries its old path through (so a renamed file's
symbols get diffed old-content vs new-content, not reported as a full
delete-then-add).

scripts.deletion-report.git_show.docstring: Content of rel_path at `base`, or None if it didn't exist there.

scripts.deletion-report.changed_old_line.docstring:
[(start, end)] line ranges in the OLD (base) version of rel_path that
this diff touches — parsed from unified=0 hunk headers, used to confirm a
symbol whose name survived the edit was actually touched, not just
re-emitted identically.

scripts.deletion-report.diff_file_symbol.docstring:
Returns (deleted, added, confirmed_modified, pre_spans_by_symbol) for
one changed file. pre_spans_by_symbol carries start/end lines so callers
can report file+line for deleted symbols.
